title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. It can lead to data encryption, service disruptions, financial losses, and potential data exfiltration. The use of compromised CI/CD pipelines increases the risk of widespread and rapid deployment.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strict access controls, multi-factor authentication (MFA), and regular security audits.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed and stored offline or in secure, immutable storage to mitigate ransomware impact.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and monitor for unusual activity, especially in cloud environments.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk, but widespread adoption could lead to attackers exploiting potential flaws in the tool or using it to identify vulnerabilities in systems that are not properly secured.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the security of the tool before integrating it into workflows.
Monitor for updates or reports of vulnerabilities in the tool itself.
Educate teams on secure dependency management practices to complement the tool's usage.

title: A vulnerability has been found in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected is the function...
description: A vulnerability has been found in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected is the function ogs_sbi_client_add in the library /lib/sbi/client.c of the component NRF. The manipulation of the argument client_pool leads to denial of service. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8731.

title: A vulnerability was detected in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This affects an unknown function in the...
description: A vulnerability was detected in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This affects an unknown function in the library /lib/sbi/message.c of the component NRF. Performing a manipulation of the argument service-names/snssais results in denial of service. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Open5GS up to version 2.7.7 could allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service by exploiting the NRF component through manipulation of service-names/snssais. This could disrupt 5G network services and impact network reliability.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade Open5GS to the latest version if available, or apply any available patches.
Implement network monitoring to detect and mitigate potential exploitation attempts targeting the NRF component.
Restrict access to the affected service to trusted sources using firewall rules or access control lists.

title: The AI Engine – The Chatbot, AI Framework & MCP for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable...
description: The AI Engine – The Chatbot, AI Framework & MCP for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in version 3.4.9. This is due to missing WordPress capability enforcement in the MCP OAuth bearer-token authorization path, where any valid OAuth token causes MCP access to be granted without verifying administrator privileges. This makes it possible for authenticated (Subscriber+) attackers to invoke admin-level MCP tools and escalate privileges to Administrator.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users with minimal privileges (Subscriber+) to escalate their privileges to Administrator by exploiting improper OAuth token validation. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, website defacement, or complete control over the WordPress site.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the AI Engine – The Chatbot, AI Framework & MCP for WordPress plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily.
Restrict access to the WordPress admin panel to trusted IP addresses using firewall rules or other access control mechanisms.
Review and revoke any suspicious OAuth tokens and audit user accounts for unauthorized privilege escalations.

title: Das U-Boot before 2026.04 allows FIT (Flat Image Tree) signature verification bypass because...
description: Das U-Boot before 2026.04 allows FIT (Flat Image Tree) signature verification bypass because hashed-nodes is omitted from a hash.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-46728.

title: WordPress Plugin Backup and Restore 1.0.3 contains an arbitrary file deletion vulnerability that...
description: WordPress Plugin Backup and Restore 1.0.3 contains an arbitrary file deletion vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to delete files by manipulating parameters in AJAX requests. Attackers can send POST requests to admin-ajax.php with crafted file_name and folder_name parameters to delete arbitrary files from the WordPress installation directory.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers to delete arbitrary files from the WordPress installation directory, potentially leading to data loss, website downtime, and compromised functionality.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the WordPress Plugin Backup and Restore to the latest patched version or disable the plugin if no update is available.
Restrict access to admin-ajax.php by implementing strong authentication and authorization measures.
Regularly back up critical files and databases to ensure recovery in case of file deletion.

title: Quick.CMS 6.7 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability in the sliders form that allows...
description: Quick.CMS 6.7 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability in the sliders form that allows authenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts by submitting XSS payloads through the sDescription parameter. Attackers can craft CSRF forms targeting the admin.php?p=sliders-form endpoint to execute arbitrary JavaScript in victim browsers when the form is submitted.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47981.

title: WP Learn Manager 1.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows...
description: WP Learn Manager 1.1.2 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts through the fieldtitle parameter. Attackers can submit POST requests to the jslm_fieldordering page with XSS payloads in the fieldtitle field to execute arbitrary JavaScript when administrators view the field ordering interface.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious JavaScript, potentially leading to session hijacking, data theft, or administrative account compromise when administrators access the affected interface.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using WordPress
recommended_actions: Update WP Learn Manager to the latest patched version or apply any available security patches addressing CVE-2021-47975.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious POST requests targeting the fieldtitle parameter.
Conduct a security audit of WordPress plugins and remove or replace outdated or vulnerable plugins.

title: My Notes Safe 5.3 contains a denial of service vulnerability that allows attackers to crash the...
description: My Notes Safe 5.3 contains a denial of service vulnerability that allows attackers to crash the application by pasting excessively long character strings into note fields. Attackers can generate a payload containing 350000 repeated characters and paste it twice into a new note to trigger an application crash.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47971.

title: CouchCMS 2.2.1 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers...
description: CouchCMS 2.2.1 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript by uploading malicious SVG files through the file upload functionality. Attackers can upload SVG files containing embedded script tags to the browse.php endpoint, which are then executed in users' browsers when the files are accessed or previewed.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript in users' browsers, potentially leading to session hijacking, data theft, or further exploitation within the application. This could compromise sensitive user information and application integrity.
recommended_actions: Update CouchCMS to the latest version if a patch is available to address CVE-2021-47955.
Implement strict file type validation to prevent malicious SVG uploads and sanitize user inputs.
Educate users and administrators about the risks of XSS vulnerabilities and monitor for suspicious activity in file uploads.

description: Kite 4.2.0.1 U1 contains an unquoted service path vulnerability in the KiteService Windows service that allows local attackers to escalate privileges by exploiting the service binary path. Attackers can place a malicious executable in the Program Files directory to be executed with LocalSystem privileges when the service starts.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2020-37247.

title: python jsonpickle 2.0.0 contains a remote code execution vulnerability that allows attackers to...
description: python jsonpickle 2.0.0 contains a remote code execution vulnerability that allows attackers to execute arbitrary Python commands by deserializing malicious JSON payloads containing py/repr objects. Attackers can craft JSON strings with py/repr directives that invoke the eval function during deserialization to execute system commands and arbitrary code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47952.

title: WordPress Plugin Anti-Malware Security and Bruteforce Firewall 4.20.59 contains a directory...
description: WordPress Plugin Anti-Malware Security and Bruteforce Firewall 4.20.59 contains a directory traversal vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to read arbitrary files by manipulating the file parameter. Attackers can send requests to the duplicator_download action via admin-ajax.php with path traversal sequences to access sensitive system files outside the intended directory.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2021-47977.

title: CVE-2026-7258 - In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.
description: In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.6, some functions, including urldecode(), pass signed char to ctype functions (like isxdigit()). On the systems with default signed char and optimized table-lookup ctype functions - such as NetBSD - this can lead to accessing array with negative offset, which can trigger a denial of service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7258.

title: CVE-2026-6735 - In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, 8.5.* before 8.5.6, d
description: In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, 8.5.* before 8.5.6, due to improper sanitation of user data, it allows an attacker to compose an URL, which will cause the target to execute arbitrary JavaScript code (XSS) on the target's machine when the target is viewing the PHP-FPM status page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript code (XSS) by exploiting improper sanitation of user data in PHP-FPM status pages. This could lead to data theft, session hijacking, or other malicious activities, particularly if the PHP-FPM status page is exposed to the internet.
recommended_actions: Update PHP to the latest patched versions (8.2.31, 8.3.31, 8.4.21, 8.5.6) immediately to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the PHP-FPM status page by implementing IP whitelisting, VPN access, or other access control mechanisms.
Regularly audit and sanitize user inputs to prevent exploitation of similar vulnerabilities in the future.

title: CVE-2026-6722 - In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.
description: In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.6, the SOAP extension's object deduplication mechanism stores pointers to PHP objects in a global map without incrementing their reference counts. When an apache:Map node contains duplicate keys, processing the second entry overwrites the first in the temporary result map, freeing the original PHP object while its stale pointer remains in the map. A subsequent href reference to the freed node can copy the dangling pointer into the result. As PHP string allocations can reclaim the freed memory region, an attacker with control over the SOAP request body can exploit this use-after-free to achieve remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in PHP's SOAP extension could allow attackers to exploit use-after-free conditions, potentially leading to remote code execution, denial of service, or data corruption. The flaw poses a critical risk to web applications relying on PHP for backend processing, especially those utilizing the SOAP extension for XML-based communication.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to the latest patched versions of PHP (8.2.31, 8.3.31, 8.4.21, or 8.5.6) to mitigate the vulnerability.
Conduct a comprehensive review of applications using the SOAP extension to identify and address potential misuse or exposure.
Implement robust monitoring and intrusion detection systems to identify any exploitation attempts targeting this vulnerability.

title: CVE-2025-14179 - In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.
description: In PHP versions 8.2.* before 8.2.31, 8.3.* before 8.3.31, 8.4.* before 8.4.21, and 8.5.* before 8.5.6, the PDO Firebird driver improperly handles NUL bytes when preparing SQL queries. During token-by-token query construction, a string token containing a NUL byte is copied via strncat(), which stops at the NUL byte, dropping the closing quote and causing subsequent SQL tokens to be interpreted as part of the string. This allows SQL injection when attacker-controlled values are quoted via PDO::quote() and embedded in SQL statements.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2025-14179.

title: CVE-2026-8224 - A vulnerability was determined in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Affected by this issue is the function pcf_se
